ccel.title: NPNF-212. Leo the Great, Gregory the Great

source.title: NPNF-212. Leo the Great, Gregory the Great

title: To Januarius, Bishop.

source.title: NPNF-212. Leo the Great, Gregory the Great

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: Gregory to Januarius, Bishop of Caralis (Cagliari).

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: We think indeed that thy position may in itself be enough to compel thee to be instant in the fulfilment of pious duties.

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: But, lest remissness of any kind should intervene to abate thy zeal, we have thought it right to exhort thee especially with regard to them.

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: Now it has come to our knowledge that your Stephen, when departing this life, by his last will and testament directed a monastery to be founded.

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: But it is said that his desire is so far unaccomplished owing to the delay of the honourable lady Theodosia, his heiress.

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: Wherefore we exhort thy Fraternity to pay the utmost attention to this matter, and admonish the above-named lady, to the end that within a year’s space she may establish a monastery as has been directed, and construct everything without dispute according to the will of the departed.

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: But if she should put off the completion of the design out of negligence or artfulness (as, for instance, if she is unable to found it in the place that had been appointed, and it is thought fit that it be placed elsewhere, and the matter is neglected through the intervening delay), then we desire that it be built by the diligence of thy Fraternity, and that, all things being set in order, the effects and revenues that have been left be appropriated by thee to this venerable place.

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: For so thou wilt both escape condemnation for remissness before the awful Judge, and, in accordance with our most religious laws, wilt be accomplishing with episcopal zeal the pious wishes of the departed, which had been disregarded.
